key_insight: TACT is a self-supervised, time-aware transformer that learns longitudinal patient representations from irregular EHRs by combining explicit temporal modeling with contrastive learning and medical hierarchies.
clinical_relevance: By better capturing disease trajectories and patient subgroups, TACT could support precision oncology efforts such as phenotype discovery and risk stratification from real-world clinical data.
method: Explicit temporal modeling of medical events
Sampling-based data augmentation using hierarchical diagnosis and medication taxonomies
evidence_signal: In a large real-world dataset, TACT outperformed two time-aware transformer baselines on patient representation and event embedding metrics.
watch_item: Performance is shown on retrospective EHR data, so real-world clinical utility and generalizability will need external validation across hospitals and coding systems.
paper_title: Time-Aware Contrastive Transformer for Longitudinal Patient Representation Learning
